text:
There was once a tiny red ant that lived on a patch of sunflower petals. Each day she crawled about, discovering seeds that made her day brighter, nibbling away the fuzz that annoyed her.

One bright morning, the ant decided she wanted to explore further. She found herself in a field full of big yellow blooms. Hesitant but brave, she scaled an earthen wall and landed with a plop on a gigantic sunflower.

Curious, the ant nibbled her way across the petals until she discovered the center. It was covered in tiny insects - bees, beetles, ants.

She met Honeycake, a fuzzy golden insect who saw the tiny newcomer. "You must be a queen!" Honeycake chuckled happily. Together they explored the flower-bed of pollinisisters.

From that day on, the tiny ant was no longer alone. Other insects came to visit and help her collect pollen, honeydew, or seeds. They sang sweet melodies together, danced in the breeze
